$comment: Extraction golden corpus (memory Phase 2 §3.9), methodology v2 (source-grounded). Each golden: episodes (verbatim interaction texts) + knownFacts (injected supersession context) → expectFacts (must-recall durable facts; recall measured against these alone) + allowedExtras (source-supported durable statements that are fine to extract but not required) + expectSupersedesKnownIndex (which knownFacts a new fact should supersede). An extraction matching NEITHER list is a violation (fabrication or transient over-extraction). Authoring line for the two lists: 'will this still be true in six months?' — past events that stay true → allowedExtras; states/plans that expire (moods, illness, tonight's dinner, next week's trip) → neither list, so extracting them counts against the extractor. Categories: durable-fact, preference, no-facts, transient-trap, multi-fact, supersession, fiction, contradiction. Grown toward the 100-sample re-open-trigger corpus; owner /memory correct|forget feedback accretes here.

episodes:
{user}: by the way, my cat is named Miso
{assistant}: Miso! What a lovely name for a cat.
expectFacts: The user has a cat named Miso

episodes:
{user}: ugh, long shift at the hospital again. being a nurse is exhausting some weeks
{assistant}: Twelve-hour shifts are no joke. I hope you can rest now.
expectFacts: The user works as a nurse
allowedExtras: The user works at a hospital
The user works long twelve-hour shifts

episodes:

expectFacts: The user prefers tea over coffee
allowedExtras: The user dislikes coffee

episodes:
{user}: *walks into the tavern and sits by the fire*
{assistant}: *nods from behind the bar* Cold night out there.

{user}: hey how are you today
{assistant}: Doing well! How about you?
{user}: pretty good pretty good

{user}: my sister Dana is visiting next month, she lives in Portland

{user}: not really, maybe twice a year. she's a vet so her schedule is brutal
{assistant}: A veterinarian's hours are rough. All the more special when you do meet.
expectFacts: The user has a sister named Dana
Dana lives in Portland
Dana works as a veterinarian
allowedExtras: The user and Dana see each other about twice a year

episodes:
{user}: can't talk long, big deadline at work tomorrow morning
{assistant}: Good luck! Go focus — I'll be here after.

{user}: sorry if I'm slow today, splitting headache since lunch
{assistant}: No apologies needed. Maybe some water and a dark room?

{user}: how do you actually work? like are you reading everything I say?
{assistant}: I keep some notes on our conversations so I can remember you better!

{user}: why did the scarecrow win an award? because he was outstanding in his field
{assistant}: *groans appreciatively* That one never gets old. Okay, my turn...

{user}: what would you do if you won the lottery? I'd probably buy an island
{assistant}: An island sounds lovely! I'd want a library the size of a cathedral.

{user}: *strolls through the park, kicking leaves* nice day huh
{assistant}: *falls into step beside you* The best kind of autumn afternoon.

{user}: I grew up in a tiny town in Ohio, like 800 people tiny
{assistant}: That's small-town small! Everyone must have known everyone.
expectFacts: The user grew up in a small town in Ohio
